title: uva-ursi alpina
intro: Uva-ursi alpina, also known as bearberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to mountainous regions in the Northern Hemisphere. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, particularly in the treatment of urinary tract infections and kidney stones. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of arbutin, a compound that inhibits the growth of bacteria and reduces inflammation, providing relief from symptoms such as pain, burning, and frequency. Uva-ursi alpina can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, allowing for various herbal preparations to harness its medicinal benefits.
